agent_output: {"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Bird requires leaderboard reads to reflect score changes within 100ms with no stale ranks. MJ's architecture routes all reads through a Fastly CDN cache with a 30-second TTL. Pippen explicitly confirms that with a 30s TTL, reads may be up to 30 seconds stale, directly violating Bird's 100ms consistency rule. These two requirements are mutually exclusive as specified: you cannot serve a 30-second cached response and guarantee 100ms freshness simultaneously.","agents_involved":["Bird","MJ","Pippen"],"options":["Option A: Drop CDN for leaderboard reads entirely. Serve directly from Redis. Satisfies Bird's 100ms rule but Pippen warns Redis will be overwhelmed at 50k concurrent viewers — requires horizontal scaling (Redis Cluster or read replicas) to survive peak load.","Option B: Relax Bird's 100ms consistency rule to tolerate up to 30s staleness for the general audience. Reserve the real-time 100ms guarantee for a smaller set of clients (e.g., players themselves, tournament admins) served directly from Redis, while spectators get the CDN-cached view. This splits the read path into two tiers.","Option C: Keep CDN but drastically reduce TTL (e.g., 1-2 seconds) and use stale-while-revalidate. This still violates the strict 100ms rule but brings staleness from 30s down to low single-digit seconds. Requires Bird to formally relax the domain rule and Pippen to validate that Redis can handle the increased origin hit rate at reduced TTL.","Option D: Use WebSocket or SSE push for leaderboard updates instead of polling through CDN. Clients subscribe and receive rank changes in real time. CDN is not in the read path. Satisfies Bird's 100ms rule. Pippen must validate that the WebSocket infrastructure can handle 50k concurrent connections."],"recommendation":"Option B or D. Option B is the pragmatic choice — most spectators tolerate a few seconds of staleness, and splitting the read path keeps both the consistency guarantee (for participants) and the scalability guarantee (for spectators). Option D is the cleanest architectural fit if the team is willing to invest in push infrastructure. Either way, Bird must clarify whether the 100ms rule applies to ALL readers or only to participants, and Pippen must sign off on the operational feasibility of whichever path is chosen. This decision cannot be deferred to Shaq — he needs a single coherent spec."}],"confidence":{"level":35,"high_confidence_areas":["The contradiction is real and clearly identified by all three agents","Redis Sorted Sets are the correct data structure for the ranking operations","50k concurrent viewers is a genuine scale concern that requires a caching or push strategy"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which resolution option the team should adopt — this depends on product priorities (real-time correctness vs. cost/complexity)","Whether Bird's 100ms rule is a hard product requirement or an aspirational target that can be tiered","Whether the infrastructure budget supports WebSocket/SSE at 50k concurrent connections"],"assumptions":["Bird's 100ms rule and no-stale-reads rule were stated as hard domain requirements, not suggestions","MJ's 30s CDN TTL was chosen deliberately for the 50k scale requirement, not arbitrarily","Pippen's assessment that Redis would be overwhelmed without CDN at 50k viewers is operationally accurate"]}}
agent_output_excerpt: {"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Bird requires leaderboard reads to reflect score changes within 100ms with no stale ranks. MJ's architecture routes all reads through a Fastly CDN cache with a 30-second TTL. These two requirements are mutually exclusive as specified: you cannot serve a 30-second cached response and guarantee 100ms freshness

content.thinking:
Clear contradiction between Bird's domain rules and MJ's architecture. Let me analyze:

- Bird requires 100ms consistency
- MJ's CDN has 30s TTL
- Pippen explicitly flags this conflict

This is a contradiction. Per my stop conditions, I must NOT produce a handoff brief. I must escalate only.
